apiBoundary: external calls go through a typed client/gateway package — no inline RestTemplate/WebClient in controllers or services

build.LINT: MANUAL: no linter wired by default — add Spotless (./mvnw spotless:check) or Checkstyle, then point LINT at it

researchLayers.name: data
researchLayers.targets: @Entity/@Repository, JPA mappings, query methods, Flyway/Liquibase migrations

researchLayers.name: build
researchLayers.targets: pom.xml (or build.gradle), plugins, Spring Boot version, profiles, CI

_notes: Starter profile for a Spring Boot service (Maven). Copy to working-docs/config.json and adjust.

Gradle variant: set buildTool=gradle and swap verbs — COMPILE './gradlew classes', BUILD './gradlew bootJar -x test', UNIT_TEST './gradlew test', INTEGRATION_TEST './gradlew integrationTest', RUN './gradlew bootRun', protectedPaths drop target/ keep build/.
LINT is MANUAL until you add a formatter/linter (Spotless or Checkstyle are common). Once added, point the LINT verb at it and add it to the java change-type mapping.
apiBoundary encodes the convention that outbound I/O lives behind typed clients, not scattered through controllers/services — adjust to match your repo's CLAUDE.md.
INTEGRATION_TEST assumes @SpringBootTest / Testcontainers behind a Maven profile or a naming convention; tune the command to how your project separates slow tests.
Set jira.mode to 'mcp' if the Atlassian MCP connector is available in your Claude Code session.
